content: Skip to login   Skip to main content   Skip to chat     Personal     Bank accounts     Everyday banking with NAB   Everyday banking with NAB   Get started with a few easy steps to make the most of your everyday banking.    Keep track of your finances 24/7 with NAB internet banking   Keep it simple by managing all your banking in one place and setting up all your regular deposits and payments.    NAB Internet Banking   Do everyday banking tasks and keep track of your finances with internet banking on your computer or tablet.    The NAB app   Manage your banking on the go with our mobile banking app for your smartphone or tablet.    Make the most of your transaction account   Make sure your transaction account is ready for all your everyday banking needs.    Making your first deposit   To make your first deposit, just use your new NAB BSB and Account Number. You'll find these within NAB Internet Banking or the NAB app.    Transfer your salary   It's easy to get your salary deposited automatically into your new account. Simply provide your employer with your new BSB and Account Number or fill out and provide your employer with the salary transfer form (PDF, 246KB), opens in new window    .     Switch your regular payments   Use NAB Internet Banking to set up any direct debits and BPAY payments including utilities, phone bills or your gym membership.    How to switch payments   Make the most of your savings account   Set yourself up for success and watch your savings grow.    Set up regular deposits   Set up a regular deposit into your savings account at the start of each month. This is easy to do in NAB Internet Banking or the NAB app.    Earn bonus interest - NAB Reward Saver   Earn bonus interest each month you make no withdrawals and at least one deposit by the second last banking day    *  View Disclaimer 1 in a given month.    Earn bonus interest - NAB iSaver   Earn bonus interest each month for the first 4 months on your first account (up to $20M).    You're all done   You're now ready to bank smarter with NAB.    NAB Visa Debit card and PIN   If you've requested a new NAB Visa Debit Card, you'll receive it shortly, together with a letter explaining how to activate your new card and set your PIN. Visit nab.com.au/activate for more information.    Access ATMs   You'll be able to use your NAB Visa Debit card at NAB ATMs across Australia with no withdrawal fees.    Close your old account   It's an easy way to streamline your money management and could help you save on fees.    Watch your savings grow   If you've got a NAB savings account, get ready to sit back and watch your savings grow.    Other ways we can help   Credit cards   Compare between our credit cards to find the right one for you.    Personal loans   You can use our personal loans to get the car you want, consolidate your debts or for something else, like a new motorbike, boat, or renovation.    Home loans   Home loans for every need with tools, calculators and guides to help you choose.    Get in touch   Customer Support Tool   Solve problems quickly online with our easy-to-follow guides. Simply select a topic and we’ll direct you to the information you need.    Customer Support Tool   Contact us   Explore our personal banking contact information and get support with a wide range of products, services and topics.    View all personal banking contacts   Visit a NAB branch   Visit us in person at your nearest NAB branch or business banking centre.    Find a NAB branch   Important Information   Terms and Conditions         Disclaimer 1   To earn the bonus interest, we need to receive the deposit on or before the second last banking day of the month. A banking day is any day that isn't a weekend or an Australia-wide public holiday.    This section contains Important Information relevant to the page you are viewing, but you can't see it because you have JavaScript disabled on your browser. Please enable JavaScript and come back so you can see the complete page. It's important that you read the Important Information in this section before acting on any information on this page.    Apologies but the Important Information section you are trying to view is not displaying properly at the moment. Please refresh the page or try again later. Windows is a registered trademark of Microsoft Corporation in the United States and other countries.    Target Market Determinations for these products are available at nab.com.au/TMD.
